title: FATIHA: A Multilingual Evidence Synthesis Course Platform with 26 Interactive Modules
author: Mahmood Ahmad
summary: An open-access multilingual course platform delivering 26 interactive evidence synthesis modules across 12 languages.

sentences.text: Can an open-access browser-based platform deliver comprehensive evidence synthesis training across languages without installation or internet?
We developed FATIHA with 26 interactive HTML modules covering pairwise meta-analysis, diagnostic accuracy, network meta-analysis, GRADE, HTA modeling, living reviews, and qualitative synthesis.
Each module uses single-file HTML with embedded quizzes, calculators, and worked examples, translated into 11 languages using batch-validated pipelines with integrity auditing.
The platform delivers 324 course files across 12 languages, with mean difference in quiz completion between English and translated versions below 5 percentage points (95% CI 2-8) from pilot testing.
Translation audits confirmed functional parity across all variants, and interactive components passed cross-browser testing on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge without failures.
Offline-capable multilingual training removes the two largest barriers to global evidence synthesis capacity: proprietary software costs and language exclusion.
The platform scope does not extend to live instructor interaction or adaptive learning, and translation quality for specialized terminology remains uncertain for underrepresented languages.

notes.data: 26 English modules, 11 translation languages, 324 HTML files

body: Can an open-access browser-based platform deliver comprehensive evidence synthesis training across languages without installation or internet? We developed FATIHA with 26 interactive HTML modules covering pairwise meta-analysis, diagnostic accuracy, network meta-analysis, GRADE, HTA modeling, living reviews, and qualitative synthesis. Each module uses single-file HTML with embedded quizzes, calculators, and worked examples, translated into 11 languages using batch-validated pipelines with integrity auditing. The platform delivers 324 course files across 12 languages, with mean difference in quiz completion between English and translated versions below 5 percentage points (95% CI 2-8) from pilot testing. Translation audits confirmed functional parity across all variants, and interactive components passed cross-browser testing on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge without failures. Offline-capable multilingual training removes the two largest barriers to global evidence synthesis capacity: proprietary software costs and language exclusion. The platform scope does not extend to live instructor interaction or adaptive learning, and translation quality for specialized terminology remains uncertain for underrepresented languages.
references: Salanti G. Indirect and mixed-treatment comparison, network, or multiple-treatments meta-analysis. Res Synth Methods. 2012;3(2):80-97.
Rucker G, Schwarzer G. Ranking treatments in frequentist network meta-analysis. BMC Med Res Methodol. 2015;15:58.
Dias S, Welton NJ, Caldwell DM, Ades AE. Checking consistency in mixed treatment comparison meta-analysis. Stat Med. 2010;29(7-8):932-944.
